plan_metadata.name: Sample Stocks Update Notification Bot Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the "skip" link at the bottom left of the Visual Studio Code welcome screen to bypass the sign-in prompt.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace item in the Explorer panel within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "View Samples" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Click on the search bar in the "Samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface to input or filter items.
Type text: 'stocks update' into the search bar at the top of the "Samples" page in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Click on the "Stocks Update" sample card in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface under the "Samples" tab. The card includes descriptions such as "Bot," "TS," "Azure Functions," and "API Key." The red crosshair points to the card's center.
Click the "Create" button in the "Stocks Update" sample section within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface of Visual Studio Code. Add ${{var:app_name:stock-update}} in to env.
Click on the "Default folder" text input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog box at coordinates (266, 79) to activate and enable editing or selection of the folder path.
@assertion 'Preview README.MD' is visible
@assertion the page title contains 'Stocks Update Notification Bot'
Right-click on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code application interface to interact with the preview pane displaying documentation for the Stocks Update Notification Bot project.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu within Visual Studio Code to close the currently open tab, specifically the "Preview README.md" tab.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# File path to modify
FILE_PATH="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/stocks-update-notification-bot/env/.env.dev"

# Ensure the file exists before proceeding
if [[ -f "$FILE_PATH" ]]; then
  # Replace the specified string in the file
  sed -i 's|TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_ENDPOINT=.*|TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_ENDPOINT=https://www.alphavantage.co|' "$FILE_PATH"
  exit 0
else
  echo "Error: File not found at $FILE_PATH"
  exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code @code replace \"TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_ENDPOINT=\" with \"TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_ENDPOINT=\"https://www.alphavantage.co\"\" in file \\\"/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/stocks-update-notification-bot/env/.env.dev\\\"\""

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Define the file path
FILE="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/stocks-update-notification-bot/env/.env.dev.user"

# Verify the file exists
if [[ ! -f "$FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Error: File $FILE does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Replace the text
sed -i 's/TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY=/TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY=demo/g' "$FILE"

# Check if the replacement was successful and output the result
grep "TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY=" "$FILE"
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY=demo

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code replace \"TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY=\" with \"TEAMSFX_API_ALPHAVANTAGE_API_KEY=\"demo\"\" in file \\\"/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/stocks-update-notification-bot/env/.env.dev.user\\\"\""
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Type the text "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" into the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" option in the command palette of Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'launch remote(chrome)' into the "Command Palette" in the Visual Studio Code interface, with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active.

Press the Enter key to select the "Launch Remote (Chrome)" option from the IntelliSense dropdown in the Visual Studio Code terminal or search bar interface, specifically in the context of debugging configurations.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft account login interface.
Click the "Yes" button in the Microsoft login prompt to confirm staying signed in.
Click the "Add" button in the pop-up window for the TeamsConversationBot app on the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" dialog of the TeamsConversationBot interface within Microsoft Teams.
@assertion "stocks-update-notif-botdev" exist
@assertion that "Microsoft Corporation" appears in the bot response and all data shown in the image is rendered correctly.
